title: Lachemilla guatemalensis
taxonomy: Lachemilla guatemalensis is classified in the family Rosaceae. This family is commonly known as the rose family. It is further classified into the genus Lachemilla, which is a group of small flowering plants. The genus Lachemilla is part of the subfamily Rosoideae, which includes plants like roses and strawberries. Lachemilla guatemalensis is a species within this classification.
uses: Lachemilla guatemalensis has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes, including treating digestive issues and skin problems. Its leaves and stems are rich in antioxidants and may have anti-inflammatory properties. In some regions, the plant is used to treat fever and cough. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Lachemilla guatemalensis for medicinal purposes.

intro: Lachemilla guatemalensis, also known as the Guatemalan rockjasmine, is a small, flowering plant native to Central America, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and creeping habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and irritable bowel syndrome. The bioactive constituents of Lachemilla guatemalensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for a range of health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and stress relief.
